caption: Base Event
description: The base event is a generic and concrete event. It also defines a set of attributes available in most event classes. As a generic event that does not belong to any event category, it could be used to log events that are not otherwise defined by the schema. The canonical occurrence timestamp for every event is time_dt (RFC 3339). The epoch-millis time field is optional and retained for backward compatibility.
name: base_event

cloud.description: Cloud provider and region context when the event originates from or pertains to a cloud environment.

compute_environment.description: The runtime environment for serverless, container, or edge workloads related to this event.

device_detail.description: Extended device context (interfaces, posture, risk). Use with device when producers capture detailed host or inventory data.

trace.description: Distributed trace context (e.g., OpenTelemetry) when the event participates in a traced request.

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.

end_time.description: The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in the aggregate event.

ontology_refs.description: Optional cross-references to external ontologies (e.g., D3FEND, STIX, UCO) for interoperability and analytics.

parent_event_uid.description: The unique identifier of the parent event that caused or triggered this event. Enables lightweight causality chains (OTel trace alignment).

related_hashes.description: A denormalized array of all file and content hashes in the event, for optimized IoC matching in columnar storage.

related_ips.description: A denormalized array of all IP addresses in the event, for optimized point-lookup queries in columnar storage.

related_users.description: A denormalized array of all usernames in the event, for optimized point-lookup queries in columnar storage.

start_time.description: The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included in the aggregate event.

time.description: Optional event timestamp as epoch milliseconds. The default and required occurrence time is time_dt; populate this only when a pipeline or consumer still expects epoch millis.

time_dt.description: The default event occurrence timestamp in RFC 3339 format. Producers must set this field; it is the canonical event time in v2.0.
